description: Evaluates how the organization identifies, assesses, treats, governs, and reports enterprise risks—including risk appetite, registers, assessment methods, treatment plans, governance forums, and integration with security, compliance, and third-party risk.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: The enterprise risk management function is established, funded, and operating effectively with clear scope, stakeholders, and measurable outcomes.
evaluation_focus: Documented ERM charter and scope
Operating model, stakeholders, and delivery cadence
Continuous improvement and stakeholder feedback

capabilities.name: Enterprise Risk Management Program
capabilities.description: A formal enterprise risk management program exists with defined objectives, scope across the organization, and alignment to regulatory and business expectations.
evaluation_focus: Documented ERM program definition or charter
Program scope across business units and functions
Program leadership, sponsorship, and reporting line

capabilities.name: Risk Appetite and Tolerance
capabilities.description: The organization defines, approves, and uses risk appetite and tolerance statements to guide decisions, investments, and escalations.
evaluation_focus: Documented risk appetite and tolerance statements
Board or executive approval and communication
Evidence that appetite informs prioritization and escalations

capabilities.name: Risk Taxonomy and Risk Register
capabilities.description: A consistent risk taxonomy and maintained risk register capture enterprise risks with owners, ratings, and status tracking.
evaluation_focus: Defined risk categories and taxonomy
Central or federated risk register with ownership
Refresh cadence and quality controls for register data

capabilities.name: Risk Assessment Methodology
capabilities.description: Standardized methods (qualitative, quantitative, or hybrid) are used to assess inherent and residual risk with consistent scoring criteria.
evaluation_focus: Documented assessment methodology and scoring scales
Application across key risk domains and business units
Calibration, peer review, or validation of assessments

capabilities.name: Risk Treatment and Mitigation Planning
capabilities.description: Identified risks have treatment decisions (accept, mitigate, transfer, avoid) with action plans, owners, and target dates.
evaluation_focus: Documented treatment decisions per material risk
Mitigation plans with owners and milestones
Tracking of treatment effectiveness and closure

capabilities.name: Risk Governance and Accountability
capabilities.description: Governance bodies, roles, and escalation paths provide oversight for enterprise risk decisions and exceptions.
evaluation_focus: Defined risk committees or governance forums
Documented RACI for risk decisions and escalations
Accountability for risk acceptance and exceptions

capabilities.name: Risk Reporting and Executive Communication
capabilities.description: Risk information is reported to leadership and the board on a defined cadence using KPIs, KRIs, and heat maps that drive action.
evaluation_focus: Defined risk reporting pack and audience
Executive and board reporting cadence
Evidence that reporting influences decisions and funding

capabilities.name: Integration with Security, Compliance, and Third-Party Risk
capabilities.description: ERM integrates inputs from information security, compliance, and third-party risk programs into a unified enterprise view.
evaluation_focus: Linkage between ERM and security risk processes
Incorporation of compliance and audit findings into risk view
Third-party and vendor risk feeding enterprise risk reporting

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
